messages.content:
An employee sets up an automation that transfers files in a specific folder on their PC to a remote drive for archiving, provided the files are more than six months old.

Which quality of automation is described in this example?


intrusive


complex


cumulative


rule-based


I don't know this yet.

The quality of automation described in this example is "rule-based." The automation is based on a specific rule or condition (files being more than six months old) and automatically carries out the transfer process when the condition is met.

What describes Accenture's approach to automation?


human-centered


cloud-centered


machine-centered


intelligence-centered


I don't know this yet.

Accenture's approach to automation is described as "human-centered." Accenture emphasizes the integration of automation technologies with human capabilities, focusing on augmenting human workers rather than replacing them. The approach aims to enhance productivity, efficiency, and decision-making by combining the strengths of humans and machines.

What is the first step to approaching automation?


aligning automation with business strategy


speeding up current operating procedures


implementing new business processes


installing applications used to develop automation


I don't know this yet.

The first step to approaching automation is "aligning automation with business strategy." Before implementing automation, it is essential to consider how it aligns with the overall goals and objectives of the business. This involves identifying specific areas where automation can bring the most value and contribute to the strategic direction of the organization.

Which describes the Paradox of Automation?


The more human involvement in an automated system decreases, the more important human involvement becomes.


The degree to which a system has been automated has no effect on the level of human involvement.


The more efficient an automated system becomes, the more human involvement is required.


The more human involvement in an automated system decreases, the less important human involvement becomes.


I don't know this yet.

The Paradox of Automation is described as: "The more efficient an automated system becomes, the more human involvement is required." It means that as automation improves and takes over various tasks, humans are required to intervene, monitor, and manage the system to ensure its proper functioning and address any unexpected situations. This paradox highlights the ongoing need for human involvement and intervention, even in highly automated processes.

To resolve client queries, customer support professionals must gather customer data from a variety of sources, such as databases, phone calls, email, and online chats. This is time-consuming and can negatively impact the customer journey.

What is something Intelligent Automation can do to improve this process?


automatically logging in to various applications


routing client requests to the appropriate employees


populating forms in one application with data from another


enabling the analysis of unstructured data


I don't know this yet.

Intelligent Automation can improve the process of gathering customer data by "populating forms in one application with data from another." This involves automating the transfer of relevant customer data from various sources, such as databases, phone calls, email, and online chats, into the appropriate forms or systems. By automating this data population process, customer support professionals can save time and reduce the likelihood of errors or inconsistencies in the data, thereby enhancing the overall customer journey.

What is an example of something that can be done through Personal Automation?


reading and analyzing the language in customer emails


creating a web-based calendar reminder for birthdays


using chat bots to answer customers' technical queries


scanning job applications to identify qualified candidates


I don't know this yet.

An example of something that can be done through Personal Automation is "creating a web-based calendar reminder for birthdays." Personal Automation refers to automation that individuals implement in their personal lives to streamline tasks and improve productivity. In this case, setting up an automated calendar reminder for birthdays can help individuals remember important dates and ensure they don't miss any celebrations.

Intelligent Automation expands on simpler forms of automation through the use of which technology?


DevOps


Artificial Intelligence


Cloud Computing


Object-Oriented Programming


I don't know this yet.

Intelligent Automation expands on simpler forms of automation through the use of "Artificial Intelligence" (AI) technology. AI enables automation to go beyond simple repetitive tasks and incorporates cognitive abilities such as natural language processing, machine learning, and computer vision. By utilizing AI, Intelligent Automation can handle complex and dynamic processes, make decisions, and adapt based on data and insights, bringing greater efficiency and intelligence to automated systems.

What is an example of a task that could be automated?


a task that involves unique decision points and approvals based on quality checks


a variable process that depends on a large amount of data and unpredictable outcomes


a recurring task that repeats the same workflow each time, but with different data


a task that executes a simple workflow once every fiscal year that takes two hours to complete


I don't know this yet.

An example of a task that could be automated is "a recurring task that repeats the same workflow each time, but with different data." This type of task involves a predictable and repetitive workflow that can be standardized and automated. By creating an automation system, the task can be executed without manual intervention, saving time and minimizing errors.

Which describes Personal Automation?


using advanced Artificial Intelligence technologies to mimic human behavior


creating sophisticated machines that can perform basic human work tasks


automating complex, unpredictable tasks using advanced software programming techniques


automating simple, repetitive tasks through user-friendly software applications


I don't know this yet.

Personal Automation refers to "automating simple, repetitive tasks through user-friendly software applications." It involves individuals implementing automation in their personal lives to streamline routine and repetitive tasks. Personal Automation can be achieved through user-friendly tools and software applications that simplify the process of automating tasks such as scheduling, reminders, data entry, and more. It aims to improve personal productivity and efficiency by eliminating manual efforts for routine activities.

What is the best way to describe automation?


making human workers fully reliant on technology to perform their job responsibilities


developing highly advanced robots that can mimic higher-level human thinking


keeping all machine-based work separate from job activities performed by humans


saving time and money by improving the quality, accuracy, and precision of tasks


I don't know this yet.

The best way to describe automation is "saving time and money by improving the quality, accuracy, and precision of tasks." Automation involves the use of technology and processes to automate repetitive, manual, or time-consuming tasks. By automating these tasks, businesses aim to enhance efficiency, reduce errors, improve productivity, and ultimately save time and money. Automation does not necessarily imply replacing human workers, but rather augmenting their capabilities and allowing them to focus on more complex or strategic aspects of their job roles.

A large organization is struggling to close the gaps in skill levels that exist between its employees and those from competing companies.

How can automation help the organization solve this problem?


by creating a greater number of automated bots than its competitors


by handling the most complex work tasks through automation


by hiring all new employees that can handle more complex tasks


by freeing up employees to train and upskill for higher-level work


I don't know this yet.

Automation can help the organization solve the skill gap problem by "freeing up employees to train and upskill for higher-level work." By automating repetitive and routine tasks, employees have more time and capacity to focus on learning new skills, acquiring knowledge, and developing expertise in areas that are critical for the organization's success. This enables employees to fill the skill gaps and compete effectively with employees from other companies who may already possess those skills. Additionally, automation can also support employees in handling complex work tasks by assisting and augmenting their capabilities.

A client asks for help planning and implementing an enterprise platform transformation, ensuring proper integration of their currently automated solutions.

Which Accenture tool would best help the Project Lead begin this process?


SynOps


myCredential


myNav


myConcerto


I don't know this yet.

The Accenture tool that would best help the Project Lead begin the process of planning and implementing an enterprise platform transformation, ensuring proper integration of currently automated solutions, is "myConcerto." myConcerto is an integrated platform developed by Accenture that supports end-to-end management and orchestration of enterprise architectural components. It provides tools for strategy, planning, portfolio management, and transformation execution, helping organizations streamline their transformation initiatives and ensure the effective integration of various automated solutions.

A regional bank implemented an automated solution to streamline their operations for receiving and processing checks. They would like to further enhance the solution to recognize signs of potential fraud and alert their security teams accordingly.

Which technology could be combined with the current solution to do this?


Robotic Process Automation


Machine Learning


Mechanization


Sentiment Analysis


I don't know this yet.

The technology that could be combined with the current solution to enhance fraud detection is "Machine Learning." Machine Learning algorithms can be trained on historical data related to check processing and fraud patterns. By analyzing incoming checks and comparing them to learned patterns, the machine learning model can identify signs of potential fraud and alert the security teams for further investigation. This integration allows the automated solution to adapt and improve its ability to detect fraudulent activities over time.

What is a Digital Worker?


an employee who can independently write code to produce automation solutions


any employee who works with computers or computerized information


an advanced bot capable of managing more complex, multi-faceted situations


a quickly-created bot that focuses on a common, repetitive task


I don't know this yet.

A "Digital Worker" refers to an advanced bot that is capable of managing more complex and multi-faceted situations. Unlike basic bots that handle repetitive tasks, a Digital Worker is designed to handle a broader range of responsibilities and can navigate more complex decision-making processes. Digital Workers often utilize artificial intelligence, machine learning, and natural language processing capabilities to understand and respond to various scenarios. They can interact with systems, extract and analyze data, and autonomously perform tasks that would typically require human intervention.

What describes Accenture's approach to automation, both internally as well as for clients?


focusing primarily on technology and tools to drive automation


implementing advanced robotics capabilities on every automation effort


using automation to make businesses and people more effective


applying a one-size-fits-all automation strategy for every client


I don't know this yet.

Accenture's approach to automation, both internally and for clients, is centered around "using automation to make businesses and people more effective." Accenture emphasizes the integration of automation technologies with human capabilities to enhance productivity, efficiency, and decision-making. Their approach focuses on leveraging automation tools and technologies to augment and empower individuals rather than replacing them. By aligning automation with business strategy and specific client needs, Accenture aims to create tailored automation solutions that drive business outcomes and improve the effectiveness of both organizations and their workforce.

How does 5G technology enhance the Internet of Things (IoT)?


by installing larger cell towers to project stronger signals


by connecting remote devices through physical cables


by supporting greater numbers of connected devices


by providing higher bandwidth for downloading data


I don't know this yet.

5G technology enhances the Internet of Things (IoT) by "supporting greater numbers of connected devices." One of the key advantages of 5G is its ability to handle a significantly larger number of simultaneous connections compared to previous generations of cellular networks. This capability is crucial for IoT applications that involve a massive number of interconnected devices. With 5G, it becomes possible to connect and manage a vast ecosystem of IoT devices seamlessly, enabling more extensive and diverse IoT deployments with improved efficiency and scalability. Additionally, 5G also offers higher bandwidth, reduced latency, and increased network reliability, further enhancing the performance and potential of IoT applications.

A group of autonomous cars are driving along the interstate. They can communicate with one another, move closer to each other, and function as one unit.

What is this called?


platooning


beamforming


connection density


massive MIMO


I don't know this yet.

The scenario described, where a group of autonomous cars communicates, moves closer, and functions as one unit, is known as "platooning." Platooning refers to a technique in which multiple vehicles travel closely together in a synchronized manner. The vehicles maintain a short distance between each other, taking advantage of their ability to communicate and coordinate movements. This approach aims to improve traffic flow, increase safety, and potentially reduce fuel consumption by minimizing aerodynamic drag.

How does lower latency benefit the users connected to a network?


by limiting network access to users with higher security credentials


by enabling more devices to connect to the network at the same time


by reducing the delay when sending and receiving data between devices


by expanding the range over which the network's signals are transmitted


I don't know this yet.

Lower latency benefits users connected to a network by "reducing the delay when sending and receiving data between devices." Latency refers to the time delay that occurs when data is transmitted between devices or sent over a network. Lower latency results in faster response times, as the delay between a user's action and the network's response is minimized. This improves the overall user experience by making applications more responsive, reducing lag in real-time communication, and enabling seamless interactions in online activities such as streaming, gaming, video conferencing, and browsing.

What underlying concept is edge computing based on?


moving computing resources closer to the source or user of data


delivering files to end users at the fastest possible speeds


using beamforming to improve the efficiency of a network


transmitting the maximum amount of data over a single connection


I don't know this yet.

The underlying concept of edge computing is based on "moving computing resources closer to the source or user of data." In edge computing, processing, storage, and analysis of data are performed closer to where the data is generated or where it is needed. Instead of sending all data to a centralized cloud server or data center, edge computing distributes the workload to local devices or edge servers located on the edge of the network. By bringing computation closer to the data source or user, edge computing reduces latency, improves real-time responsiveness, minimizes data transfer and bandwidth requirements, and enhances overall system efficiency.

A large industrial machine is able to monitor environmental conditions and quickly shut down and sound an alarm when it detects a dangerous situation.

What feature of the technology allows this to happen?


reduced latency


effective range


improved bandwidth


connection density


I don't know this yet.

The feature of the technology that allows the industrial machine to monitor and respond quickly to dangerous situations is "reduced latency." Latency refers to the delay between the transmission of data and its receipt. In this case, the machine's ability to monitor environmental conditions, process the data, and take immediate action depends on low latency. With reduced latency, the machine can detect and analyze the environmental conditions in real-time, enabling it to quickly shut down and sound an alarm when a dangerous situation is detected. This ensures a swift response and mitigates potential risks or hazards in industrial settings.

A retailer is able to track which products draw the most attention from its customers through the use of 5G-enabled motion sensors placed around the store. With this knowledge, the retailer can supply the proper amount of products to meet customer demand.

Which technology combines with 5G capabilities to allow the monitoring of shopping trends in this way?


internet of things


network slicing


platooning


beamforming


I don't know this yet.

The technology that combines with 5G capabilities to allow the monitoring of shopping trends in this way is the "Internet of Things" (IoT). IoT refers to the network of physical devices, sensors, and objects that are connected and capable of collecting and exchanging data. In this scenario, the 5G-enabled motion sensors placed around the store are part of the IoT infrastructure. These sensors collect data on customer movement and attention, which is then transmitted over the 5G network for analysis and processing. By leveraging IoT and 5G technologies together, retailers can gain valuable insights into shopping trends, optimize inventory management, and meet customer demand more efficiently.

A global clothing company is looking to create a more immersive shopping experience for customers.

What is a way the company can utilize edge computing to help the store achieve this goal?


analyzing a customer's past purchases and offering customized recommendations


installing digital codes on items that can be scanned by customers for more detail


using sensors to track a customer's browsing habits and create a virtual dressing room


setting up an online payment system to increase the speed of check-out at the store


I don't know this yet.

A way the global clothing company can utilize edge computing to create a more immersive shopping experience is by "using sensors to track a customer's browsing habits and create a virtual dressing room." By deploying sensors throughout the store and connecting them with edge computing infrastructure, the company can track customers' movements, preferences, and interactions with the clothing items. This data can be analyzed in real-time at the edge, allowing the store to create personalized virtual dressing rooms where customers can try on virtual outfits or view clothing items in different colors and styles. This immersive experience enhances customer engagement, improves decision-making, and offers a more interactive and personalized shopping environment.

Which factors have made edge computing cheaper and easier?


cloud computing and containers


quantum computing and biometrics


artificial intelligence and blockchain


lower bandwidth and higher latency


I don't know this yet.

The factors that have made edge computing cheaper and easier are "cloud computing and containers." Cloud computing has enabled the availability of scalable and cost-effective resources to support edge computing deployments. By leveraging cloud services, organizations can offload computation and storage to the cloud, making edge computing more affordable and accessible. Additionally, containers provide a lightweight and portable way to package applications and services, allowing for easier deployment and management of edge computing environments. Containers simplify the development, deployment, and scaling of applications at the edge, reducing complexity and enhancing the efficiency of edge computing solutions.

What happens after the initial rollout of a new network generation?


The previous network generation will be deactivated after a transition period of a few months.


The new network generation will continue to be developed and enhanced over its lifetime.


All devices created to work with the previous network generation must upgrade or lose service.


Development of the network is frozen until the rollout of the next network generation.


I don't know this yet.

After the initial rollout of a new network generation, "The new network generation will continue to be developed and enhanced over its lifetime." Network technologies are not stagnant but undergo continuous improvement and evolution over time. After the initial rollout, the network generation will undergo further development, refinement, and enhancements to address any issues, optimize performance, and introduce new features. This iterative process ensures that the network remains up to date, reliable, and capable of meeting the growing demands and evolving technologies of the future.

Which situation would benefit the most by using edge computing?


an offshore oil rig needs to more efficiently process data


an IT department wants to increase the capacity of its central server


a clothing company wants to track customer preferences


a theater wants to record performances for its website


I don't know this yet.
The situation that would benefit the most from using edge computing
